headline_metrics.nii: NII up 13% YoY (specific dollar NII not broken out in release)

nim_nii.summary: Net interest income grew 13% year-over-year, driven by growth across all five businesses and Legacy Franchises, partially offset by a decline in Corporate/Other. The Corporate/Other NII decline was attributed to actions taken to reduce asset sensitivity in a lower interest rate environment, suggesting NII guidance is directionally held-to-expanding at the segment level even as Corporate Treasury positioning weighs on the consolidated figure.
nim_nii.quote: Net interest income increased 13%, driven by growth across each of Citi's five businesses and Legacy Franchises, partially offset by a decline in Corporate/Other.
nim_nii.speaker: Earnings Release

credit_normalization.summary: Net credit losses rose 8% year-over-year to $2.4B, but the overall provision fell 12% YoY as ACL builds moderated sharply. The reserve-to-funded-loans ratio edged down to 2.5% from 2.7%, and management cited 'better credit performance than expected' in U.S. Consumer Cards — suggesting credit is normalizing/improving rather than deteriorating, with USCC leading the improvement.
credit_normalization.quote: Despite short-term headwinds from investments in our U.S. Consumer Cards portfolio, our resilient customer base kept fueling underlying drivers: loan growth, higher spend and better credit performance than expected.
credit_normalization.speaker: Jane Fraser, Chair and CEO

cre_reserves.summary: Commercial real estate is not explicitly broken out as a standalone reserve discussion in this release. The release covers ACL builds and releases at the segment level; no specific CRE or office CRE reserve action is identified or quantified.

deposit_costs.summary: Deposit costs are not explicitly discussed in terms of betas or rate levels. Services NII growth was driven by higher average deposit balances and deposit spreads, while Securities Services NII grew on higher balances but was partially offset by lower deposit spreads — suggesting mixed deposit spread dynamics across segments rather than a uniform directional trend.
deposit_costs.quote: The increase in net interest income was driven by higher average deposit balances and deposit spreads.
deposit_costs.speaker: Earnings Release

capital_return.summary: Citi returned ~$5.0B to shareholders in Q2 via buybacks and dividends, representing a 92% payout ratio. Management announced a 12% dividend increase and launched a $30B buyback plan, with CET1 at 12.8%, up from 12.7% last quarter. The tone is clearly one of increasing capital return.
capital_return.quote: Our growing earnings generation will allow us to increase our planned dividend by 12% and we have launched our $30 billion buyback plan.
capital_return.speaker: Jane Fraser, Chair and CEO

loan_growth.summary: End-of-period loans grew 9% YoY and 4% QoQ to $794B, driven organically by Markets, USCC, and Services. USCC also onboarded the American Airlines co-branded card portfolio in Q2 2026, which is an acquisition-driven contributor to that segment's loan growth. Banking average loans grew 5% YoY, with growth in episodic IB-related loans offsetting declines in core corporate lending balances.
loan_growth.quote: Citigroup's end-of-period loans were $794 billion at quarter end, up 9% versus the prior-year period, primarily driven by higher loans in Markets, USCC and Services.
loan_growth.speaker: Earnings Release

macro_outlook.summary: Management's tone is cautiously constructive: consumer credit is performing better than expected, revenues are at a decade high, and capital returns are increasing. However, forward-looking statements cite 'macroeconomic, geopolitical and other challenges and uncertainties, including elevated inflation and conflicts in the Middle East,' and Corporate/Other NII was deliberately reduced to cut asset sensitivity ahead of a lower rate environment — signaling some macro caution embedded in balance sheet positioning.
macro_outlook.quote: These factors include, among others, macroeconomic, geopolitical and other challenges and uncertainties, including elevated inflation and conflicts in the Middle East.
macro_outlook.speaker: Earnings Release (forward-looking statement disclaimer)

notable_qa.summary: This document is a written earnings release (8-K Ex. 99.1), not a call transcript; no Q&A session is included or available for analysis. The source explicitly notes the call transcript was not freely published on any parsable source as of the filing date.

source: Q2 2026 earnings release (8-K EX-99.1, EDGAR) -- call transcript not freely published as of 2026-07-21; quotes are from the written release
